scope.summary: Implement a named export-configuration preset system so operators and models can save and recall repeatable import/export parameter sets (format, unit scale, feature flags, seam-allowance defaults, chaos-cloth flags, UE bridge path) as reusable presets
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/export-presets creates a named export-configuration preset row (export_preset_id = EXP-PRESET-{uuid_v7}) storing format, coordinate_system, unit_scale, seam_allowance_mm, chaos_cloth_attributes, ue_bridge_path, auto_sync as a JSONB config column
POST /tailor/garments/:id/export with export_preset_id uses the stored configuration rather than requiring all parameters inline
CRUD (list, get, update, delete) for export presets is exposed via REST and as an MCP tool
Models can request a recommended preset by target_engine ('blender' | 'unreal' | 'maya' | 'physical_production') and receive the nearest matching preset
guard_authority_write enforced on all preset inserts/updates
scope.risk_if_missed: Without export presets, every export call requires the operator or model to re-specify format, unit, and engine-specific parameters. In batch production pipelines (MT-329 batch export of wardrobes) this creates configuration drift and errors as parameters are repeated across hundreds of export calls.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
